metadata.name: UW-RestClients-Core
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Core code for clients of a variety of RESTful web services at the University of Washington

modules.name: restclients_core.test
documentation: This is just a test runner for coverage

classes.name: restclients_core.models.MockHTTP

methods.name: restclients_core.models.MockHTTP.read
documentation: Returns the document body of the request.

methods.name: restclients_core.models.MockHTTP.getheader
documentation: Returns the HTTP response header field, case insensitively

An alternate object to HTTPResponse, for non-HTTP DAO
implementations to use.  Implements the API of HTTPResponse
as needed.

classes.name: restclients_core.models.CacheHTTP
documentation:
A class which implements the interface of HTTPResponse but signals to
clients that the object they are receiving is from a cache.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.service_name
documentation:
This method must be overridden to define your service's short name.

This name is used in multiple places.  The Mock DAO uses it in path
names for file, and the Django app for browsing services uses it as
part of the URL.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO._custom_headers
documentation:
This method can be overridden to add headers to a request.  For
example, a Bearer header can be added if a service uses OAuth tokens.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO._custom_response_edit
documentation:
This method allows a service to edit a response.

If you want to do this, you probably really want to use
_edit_mock_response - this method will operate on Live resources.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO._edit_mock_response
documentation:
Override this method to edit responses in mock resources.  This can be
used to ensure datetime fields have useful values relative to now,
or to provide more dynamic behavior for PUT/POST/DELETE requests.

This method should edit the response object directly.  No return value.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.get_default_service_setting
documentation:
A hook for setting useful defaults.  For example, if you have a host
your service almost always uses, you can have this method return that
value when passed 'HOST'.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.getURL
documentation: Request a URL using the HTTP method GET

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.postURL
documentation: Request a URL using the HTTP method POST.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.putURL
documentation: Request a URL using the HTTP method PUT.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.patchURL
documentation: Request a URL using the HTTP method PATCH.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.deleteURL
documentation: Request a URL using the HTTP method DELETE.

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.service_mock_paths
documentation:
If your web service client ships with mock resources, override this
method to return a list of top level paths where they can be found.

e.g. If your resource is in
/users/my/my_client/resources/client/file/hello.json
you should generate ["/users/my/my_client/resources"]

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.prometheus_duration
documentation:
Override this method if you have service-specific logic
around response times

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.DAO.prometheus_status
documentation:
Override this method to insert service-specific logic
before setting the response status code observation

e.g., If the service applies special meaning to, say, 404 response
that might not make sense to observe

classes.name: restclients_core.dao.LiveDAO

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.LiveDAO.create_pool
documentation: Return a ConnectionPool instance of given host

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.LiveDAO._get_connect_timeout
documentation:
The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a connection
attempt to a server to succeed

methods.name: restclients_core.dao.LiveDAO._get_timeout
documentation:
The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait between
consecutive READ operations for a response from the server.
Loads response objects by fetching resources from an HTTP(s) server.

classes.name: restclients_core.dao.MockDAO
documentation: Loads response objects based on file content.

classes.name: restclients_core.exceptions.DataFailureException
documentation:
This exception means there was an error fetching content
in one of the rest clients.  You can get the url that failed
with .url, the status of the error with .status, and any
message with .msg

functions.name: restclients_core.util.local_cache.set_cache_value
documentation:
If local cache is being used, this will set a cache value for the
lifetime of that cache.

functions.name: restclients_core.util.local_cache.get_cache_value
documentation: If local cache is being used, this will get the cache value, if it exists.

classes.name: restclients_core.util.local_cache.local_cache

methods.name: restclients_core.util.local_cache.local_cache.__call__
documentation: This handles function decorators + class decorators.  Needs to switch

methods.name: restclients_core.util.local_cache.local_cache.__enter__
documentation: These handle context managers, and are used by the others

A decorator or context manager that will cache some values for the
lifetime of the decorator/context manager.

functions.name: restclients_core.util.retry.retry
documentation:
Decorator function for retrying the decorated function,
using an exponential or fixed backoff.

Original: https://wiki.python.org/moin/PythonDecoratorLibrary#Retry

ExceptionToCheck: the exception to check. Can be a tuple of
    exceptions to check
tries: number of times to try (not retry) before giving up
delay: initial delay between tries in seconds
backoff: backoff multiplier
status_codes: list of http status codes to check for retrying, only applies
     when ExceptionToCheck is a DataFailureException
logger: logging.Logger instance

classes.name: restclients_core.cache.NoCache
documentation: A cache implementation that never caches.

functions.name: restclients_core.util.mock.convert_to_platform_safe
documentation:
---
param dir_file_name: a string to be processed
return: a string with all the reserved characters replaced
---

functions.name: restclients_core.util.mock.open_file
documentation: Taking in a file path, attempt to open mock data files with it.

functions.name: restclients_core.util.mock.attempt_open_query_permutations
documentation:
Attempt to open a given mock data file with different permutations of the
query parameters
